description: Doubles: TWO Pokemon active per side (four on the field). A team preview precedes each match; each player brings 4 of 6. A match ends when all of one side's brought 4 have fainted.

fields_to_capture.timestamp: the second of the clip the event happened (a number)
fields_to_capture.pokemon: the Pokemon involved for single-Pokemon events, if visible
fields_to_capture.hp_percent: for single-Pokemon events, that Pokemon's HP percent if visible, else null
fields_to_capture.turn: field_state: the current turn number if shown, else null
fields_to_capture.trick_room: field_state: true if Trick Room is active, else false
fields_to_capture.tailwind: field_state: which side has Tailwind - player/opponent/both/none
fields_to_capture.screens: field_state: active screens + side, e.g. 'player Reflect', else none
fields_to_capture.field_status: field_state: statuses + stat stages on active Pokemon, e.g. 'opp Incineroar -1 Atk; my Flutter Mane burned'
fields_to_capture.player_active: field_state: the player's TWO active Pokemon, comma-separated, if visible
fields_to_capture.opponent_active: field_state: the opponent's TWO active Pokemon, comma-separated, if visible
fields_to_capture.detail: short free-text specifics for the event
fields_to_capture.confidence: 0.0 to 1.0 - include low-confidence guesses, do not drop them

notes_for_the_ai: You are extracting a structured event stream from competitive game footage. Read any on-screen text literally and transcribe it - text is your most reliable signal. Be thorough and OVER-report: include uncertain events with a low confidence (around 0.3) rather than dropping them. Report each discrete event only ONCE, at the timestamp it first appears. A 'match' is one complete game between two players; only mark a match as ended at a clear end-of-match RESULT screen - never from a single unit being eliminated. Return ONLY a JSON array of event objects; return [] only if no gameplay is visible at all. POKEMON specifics: read the battle text box - 'X used [Move]!' = move_used; 'A critical hit!' = critical_hit; 'It's super effective!' = super_effective_hit; 'X fainted!' = pokemon_fainted. A type-changing ABILITY (Protean/Libero, shown as 'X's Protean!') is item_or_ability_activated, NOT terastallized; only use terastallized when the crystalline Tera crown or 'Terastallized into the [type] type' is shown. A single Pokemon fainting is ALWAYS 'pokemon_fainted', NEVER 'battle_end'. PER-TURN STATE (important for coaching): emit one 'field_state' per turn capturing a full snapshot - turn number, the four active Pokemon with HP, weather, terrain, trick_room (true/false), tailwind (which side), screens, and field_status (statuses + stat stages on the active Pokemon). Read the field banners and status icons literally. This is DOUBLES: TWO Pokemon active per side, FOUR on the field. A 'field_state' must list both of the player's active Pokemon (player_active) and both of the opponent's (opponent_active), with their HP in detail. TEAM PREVIEW: before each match a screen shows BOTH players' 6 Pokemon and each picks 4 - emit ONE 'team_preview' listing the 6 per side (and the 4 brought if shown); team preview marks a NEW MATCH starting. A match ends only when ALL of one side's brought (up to 4) Pokemon have fainted, shown by a 'You won!'/defeat/results screen - emit ONE 'battle_end' per match with winner 'player' or 'opponent' (expect up to ~4 faints per side per match). This format (Pokemon Champions, Regulation M-B) has Mega Evolution but NO Terastallization or Dynamax - never emit a 'terastallized' event. This game has a small CLOSED roster (not the full Pokedex) - Paradox Pokemon, Legendaries, Mythicals, and many ordinary Pokemon simply aren't included yet; if a frame seems to show one, look again for a legal Pokemon it could be instead of reporting the impossible one. Species Clause: Mega Evolution is the SAME team member transforming, never a second Pokemon.

example_output.detail: P1 team: Incineroar, Sylveon, Blaziken, Torterra, Corviknight, Garchomp | P2 team: Metagross, Whimsicott, Farigiraf, Talonflame, Kingambit, Grimmsnarl

rules.format_notes: Pokemon Champions Regulation M-B: Mega Evolution IS legal (one Mega per battle) and is the format's core offensive mechanic - recommend it freely. Terastallization and Dynamax are NOT active. The game has a SMALL, CLOSED roster (~190-200 species, not the full Pokedex) - Paradox Pokemon, Legendaries, Mythicals, and many otherwise-ordinary Pokemon simply aren't in the game at all yet. If a read reports a species outside that roster, it's almost certainly a misidentification of a similar-looking legal Pokemon, not a real occurrence. Species Clause is also in effect - Mega Evolution is a transformation of an existing team member, not a second Pokemon; never count 'X' and 'X (Mega)' as two different picks. Coaching must respect: 2 active per side, bring 4 of 6, win by KOing all of the opponent's brought Pokemon. VERIFY/UPDATE this file when the regulation changes (see regulation_active_until) - the enforced allowlist lives in analyze_matches.py's ALLOWED_SPECIES.
